meaning: adj.[生][医]异种的,异质的,异源的
examples: Advanced examples: Running a heterogenous environment
IBM has a heterogenous approach toward virtualization, with KVM one of several options.
These questions led us to experiment with how to build a hybrid cluster, a cluster consisting of heterogenous operating systems.
Techniques developed to deal with interlinked, heterogenous data acquired by massive web companies will be our main tools as the rest of us transition to digital-native operation.
